title: Threads, Atomics, Data Races, and Safe Reclamation · Invariant Recall
summary: After a delay, a release reviewer reconstructs the threaded lifetime and reclamation proof contract from partial traces, compares two near-miss implementations, and retrieves prior bounds and lifetime rules before feedback. Planned artifact: Extend the evidence record for threaded lifetime and reclamation proof after a delayed changed case. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Identify conflicting non-atomic accesses without happens-before ordering as undefined behavior.
Select atomic operations and memory order from a stated synchronization proof instead of folklore.
Publish fully initialized objects with ordering and retain their lifetime until every reader is finished.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.
